intro: Rudgea microcarpa, also known as the 'Brazilian Rosewood', is a small to medium-sized tree native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, fever, and respiratory infections.
